01_initial_request.input_data: AAI pro- and mature peptide sequences from Chenopodium quinoa, Beta vulgaris, and Amaranthus hypochondriacus
01_initial_request.user_intent: To engineer AAIs for superior selectivity and efficacy against coleopteran α-amylases and elucidate propeptide functions for biotechnological pest control
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Fold increase in specific inhibition potential against target coleopteran α-amylases

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] AAI pro- and mature peptide sequences from Chenopodium quinoa, Beta vulgaris, and Amaranthus hypochondriacus are required for engineering. [Gap] Sequences need to be retrieved from public databases to begin analysis. [Decision] To achieve sequence acquisition, chose to use NCBI Database to perform sequence retrieval on AAI genes, expecting to obtain accession numbers and sequences.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Amino acid sequences of AAIs and predicted AAI from Beta vulgaris retrieved.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sequences have been retrieved. [Gap] Sequence homology and conservation need to be assessed. [Decision] To achieve sequence alignment, chose to use Clustal Omega to perform multiple sequence alignment on BvAI and reported AAIs, expecting to obtain alignment data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Multiple sequence alignment of BvAI done with reported AAIs.

references.citation: Madeira F, et al. (2022) Nucleic Acids Res 50:W276–W279
references.relevance: Cited as reference [18] for Clustal omega tool

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sequences are aligned. [Gap] Evolutionary relationships need to be determined. [Decision] To achieve phylogenetic analysis, chose to use MEGA-X software to perform phylogenetic analysis on aligned sequences, expecting to obtain phylogenetic tree.

parameters.outgroup: Actin from Dictyostelium discoidum (UniProtKB ID: P07830)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Phylogenetic analysis performed, BvAI belongs to the clade of AAIs.

references.citation: Kumar S, et al. (2018) Mol Biol Evol 35:1547–1549
references.relevance: Cited as reference [19] for MEGA-X software

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Phylogenetic analysis is complete. [Gap] Secondary structural features need visualization. [Decision] To achieve structure illustration, chose to use EsPript3 server to perform secondary structure illustration on AAI sequences, expecting to obtain structural feature diagrams.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Secondary structural features illustrated.

references.citation: Robert X, Gouet P (2014) Nucleic Acids Res 42:W320–W324
references.relevance: Cited as reference [20] for EsPript3 server

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sequences are characterized. [Gap] Recombinant proteins need to be generated for testing. [Decision] To achieve protein production, chose to use pET28a vector to perform cloning of ORFs into expression vector, expecting to obtain recombinant plasmids.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Amplicons cloned in expression vector pET28a.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Plasmids are constructed. [Gap] Proteins need to be expressed in host cells. [Decision] To achieve protein expression, chose to use E. coli SHuffle T7 cells to perform transformation and induction, expecting to obtain recombinant proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Proteins expressed in E. coli SHuffle T7 cells.

references.citation: Lobstein J, et al. (2012) Microb Cell Fact 11:1
references.relevance: Cited as reference [21] for SHuffle T7 cells

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteins are expressed. [Gap] Proteins need purification. [Decision] To achieve purification, chose to use Ni-NTA resin to perform affinity chromatography on cell lysate, expecting to obtain purified recombinant proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Protein eluted with buffer containing 250 mM imidazole.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteins are purified. [Gap] Molecular weight needs confirmation. [Decision] To achieve MW estimation, chose to use MALDI-TOF/TOF 5800 system to perform molecular weight analysis on recombinant proteins, expecting to obtain mass spectra.

parameters.matrix: 10 mg sinapinic acid per ml of 50% ACN and 0.1% TFA

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Molecular weight of AsAI_P (9694 Da) and CqAI_P (11761 Da) estimated.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteins are characterized. [Gap] Inhibitory potential needs evaluation. [Decision] To achieve activity assessment, chose to use DNSA assay to perform inhibitory assays against recombinant Tribolium castaneum α-amylase (TcAmy), expecting to obtain IC50 values.

parameters.substrates: Various amylases (CcAmy, HaAmy, PPA, HPA, etc.)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: IC50 values determined for mature and precursor α-AIs.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial inhibition data obtained. [Gap] Chimeric inhibitors need to be designed in silico. [Decision] To achieve chimera design, chose to use In silico combination to perform library generation by exchanging propeptides and mature peptides, expecting to obtain chimera sequences.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Library of protein chimeras made by exchanging propeptides and mature peptides.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Chimera sequences designed. [Gap] 3D structures of chimeras need prediction. [Decision] To achieve structure prediction, chose to use I-TASSER server to perform three-dimensional structural modeling on precursor and chimeric AAIs, expecting to obtain 3D models.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Three-dimensional structural models of precursor and chimeric AAIs predicted.

references.citation: Roy A, et al. (2010) Nat Protoc 5:725–738
references.relevance: Cited as reference [24] for I-TASSER server

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Inhibitor models ready. [Gap] Target amylase structures need modeling. [Decision] To achieve target modeling, chose to use SWISS-MODEL to perform homology modeling on TcAmy and CcAmy, expecting to obtain amylase structures.

parameters.template: TMA crystal structure (PDB ID: 1CLV, chain A)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: TcAmy, CcAmy, and BvAI_M modeled.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Inhibitor and amylase models ready. [Gap] Binding interactions need prediction. [Decision] To achieve docking, chose to use Z-dock server to perform inhibitor binding prediction with target amylases, expecting to obtain docked complexes.

parameters.selection: Catalytic residues of α-amylases and corresponding inhibitor binding residues

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Inhibitor binding with target amylases predicted.

references.relevance: Cited as reference [26] for Z-dock server

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Docked complexes generated. [Gap] Complexes need energy minimization. [Decision] To achieve minimization, chose to use UCSF Chimera to perform energy minimization on docked complexes, expecting to obtain minimized structures.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Energy of docked complexes minimized.

references.citation: Pettersen EF, et al. (2004) J Comput Chem 25:1605–1610
references.relevance: Cited as reference [27] for UCSF Chimera

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Complexes minimized. [Gap] Interaction energies need calculation for selection. [Decision] To achieve energy calculation, chose to use PDBe-PISA server to perform interaction energy calculation on complexes, expecting to obtain binding energies.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Interaction energy calculated, CqBvAI selected based on enhanced binding potential.

references.citation: Pires DEV, et al. (2014) Nucleic Acids Res 42:314–319
references.relevance: Cited as reference [28] for PDBe-PISA interaction calculation context

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CqBvAI selected in silico. [Gap] Chimera needs wet-lab construction. [Decision] To achieve chimera construction, chose to use Overlap extension PCR to perform criss-cross construct generation (AsCqAI and CqBvAI), expecting to obtain chimera amplicons.

parameters.cycles: 5 PCR cycles for annealing and extension

02_agent_trajectory.observation: AsCqAI and CqBvAI amplicons obtained and cloned.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Chimeras constructed. [Gap] Chimera inhibitory potential needs validation. [Decision] To achieve validation, chose to use DNSA assay to perform inhibitory assays on CqBvAI against TcAmy, expecting to obtain IC50 values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: CqBvAI IC50 value reduced to 13.51 μM (7.6 fold higher potency than CqAI_M).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Chimera engineering successful. [Gap] Further improvement via mutagenesis desired. [Decision] To achieve mutagenesis design, chose to use DUET server to perform site-saturation mutagenesis on AhAI, expecting to obtain stability predictions (ddG).

parameters.mutations: Each of 32 amino acids replaced with remaining 19 amino acids

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 171 stable mutations identified, 6 mutants (N6M, etc.) selected.

references.citation: Pires DEV, et al. (2014) Nucleic Acids Res 42:314–319
references.relevance: Cited as reference [28] for DUET server

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mutants designed. [Gap] Mutant affinity needs assessment. [Decision] To achieve affinity assessment, chose to use Z-DOCK to perform docking of mutants with TMA, TcAmy, and HPA, expecting to obtain interaction energies.

parameters.selection: Mutant complexes with higher free energy than wild-type

02_agent_trajectory.observation: N6M showed highest affinity for binding (lowest ΔG).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] N6M selected computationally. [Gap] Complex stability needs simulation. [Decision] To achieve stability analysis, chose to use GROMACS to perform 10 ns molecular dynamics simulation on TcAmy-AI complexes, expecting to obtain RMSD/RMSF plots.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Interactions remain stable, N6M showed stronger affinity than wild-type.

references.citation: Jo S, et al. (2008) J Comput Chem 29:1859–1865
references.relevance: Cited as reference [29] for CHARMM-GUI/GROMACS context

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] N6M validated in silico. [Gap] Mutant needs wet-lab generation. [Decision] To achieve mutant generation, chose to use Quikchange Lightning kit to perform site-directed mutagenesis on AhAI, expecting to obtain mutant plasmids.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Mutated plasmids transformed and sequence characterized.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mutants generated. [Gap] Mutant inhibitory potential needs final validation. [Decision] To achieve final validation, chose to use DNSA assay to perform inhibitory assays on AhAI_N6M against TcAmy, expecting to obtain IC50 values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: AhAI_N6M IC50 1.358 μM, around three times more potent than wild-type AhAI.

chimera_potency_increase_CqBvAI_vs_CqAI_.interpretation: CqBvAI chimera showed 7.6 fold higher inhibition potential than parental CqAI_M.
CqBvAI chimera showed 4.26 fold higher inhibition potential than parental CqAI_P.

mutant_potency_increase_N6M_vs_WT.interpretation: AhAI_N6M mutant is around three times more potent than wild-type AhAI.

mutant_IC50_N6M.interpretation: IC50 value of AhAI_N6M against TcAmy.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The initial objective was achieved. Domain shuffling (CqBvAI) and site-saturation mutagenesis (AhAI_N6M) successfully engineered AAIs with superior selectivity and efficacy against coleopteran α-amylases, demonstrating significant fold increases in specific inhibition potential.
